name: Auckland
summary: Kia ora! Auckland is New Zealand's biggest city, built on an isthmus between two sparkling harbours and 53 sleepy volcanoes. Maori call it Tamaki Makaurau, meaning 'desired by many', and you'll see why — sailboats everywhere, a tower that pokes the clouds, and a forest-covered volcano just a ferry ride from the wharf.
funFacts: Auckland sits on top of 53 volcanoes! The youngest, Rangitoto Island, popped up from the sea only about 600 years ago and Maori people actually saw it erupt.
The Sky Tower is 328 metres tall, the highest building in New Zealand, with a glass floor at the top so you can stare straight down at tiny cars.
Auckland is nicknamed the 'City of Sails' because more sailboats live here per person than almost anywhere else on Earth — they speckle the harbour like confetti.
More Pacific Islander people live in Auckland than in any other city in the world, which means amazing food, music, and dance from across the Pacific Ocean.
The whole city sits on a skinny strip of land less than 2 km wide between two seas — the Pacific Ocean on one side and the Tasman Sea on the other!
thingsToDo: Ride the lift up the Sky Tower and tiptoe across its see-through glass floor, 186 metres above the streets — count the boats in Waitemata Harbour below.
Catch a 25-minute ferry from the downtown wharf to Rangitoto Island, then hike the lava trail to its 260-metre summit through pohutukawa forest.
Meet kiwi, tuatara and cheeky kea at Auckland Zoo's Te Wao Nui, the only zone in the world dedicated entirely to native New Zealand wildlife.
Walk through the shark tunnel and meet Antarctic penguins at SEA LIFE Kelly Tarlton's, a sea-life aquarium built inside old underground sewage tanks.
Explore Maori treasures, a real war canoe, and the giant volcano gallery at Auckland War Memorial Museum, then watch a live haka performance.
Stroll the Wynyard Quarter and Viaduct Harbour from Queens Wharf for fish and chips, then pop into the Maritime Museum to try sailing simulators.

localWord.meaning: Hello, thanks, or 'be well' — a friendly te reo Maori greeting used everywhere in New Zealand.

images.alt: Auckland's tall Sky Tower glowing against the city's night sky.
The big steel Auckland Harbour Bridge stretching across the blue water.
Rangitoto Island's perfect volcano cone rising out of the sea.
Bright red pohutukawa flowers, New Zealand's Christmas tree blooms.
The grand stone Auckland War Memorial Museum on a sunny day.
